mnemonics: LeNet Alexes VGG, Googles Inception, ResNet Revolution, Efficiently Nets Evolve
keyPoints: LeNet-5 pioneered CNNs for digit recognition, using convolution, pooling, and fully connected layers.
AlexNet's success in ImageNet 2012 popularized deep CNNs, using ReLU and dropout.
Architectures evolved with deeper networks (VGG), inception modules (GoogleNet), skip connections (ResNet), and efficient mobile designs (MobileNet).
commonMistakes: Assuming all CNNs use the same activation function (e.g., early used tanh/sigmoid, modern use ReLU).
Overlooking the importance of skip connections (e.g., ResNet) in enabling very deep networks.
Confusing pooling layers (downsampling) with convolutional layers (feature extraction).
